_convention._note: KEY NAMING RULES — read before adding keys:
_convention._1: Use camelCase for keys: commentSubmit not comment_submit
_convention._2: Group by page/feature namespace: nav, home, blog, about
_convention._3: Shared UI primitives go in 'common', NOT in feature namespaces
_convention._4: en.json is source of truth — zh.json must mirror structure exactly
_convention._5: Run 'npm run i18n:check' before committing to catch zombie keys

home.title: Senior Database & Integration Administrator
home.subtitle: Connecting systems. Governing data. Automating complexity.
home.bio: I design and maintain enterprise integrations between <netsuite>NetSuite</netsuite>, <boomi>Boomi</boomi>, and modern data platforms — keeping critical business processes reliable, observable, and fast.
home.noProjectsNote: Currently working on something great — check back shortly.

blog.title: Blog
blog.subtitle: Thoughts on engineering, systems, and building things.
blog.commentCount: {count, plural, =0 {No comments yet} one {# comment} other {# comments}}
blog.commentDisclaimer: Comments are reviewed before appearing. No spam, no selling your email.
blog.commentSubmitSuccessNote: It will appear after review. Thanks for reading.

about.title: Jack Deng
about.subtitle: Senior Database & Integration Administrator
about.bio1: Hi, I'm Jack — a database and integration specialist based in Australia, focused on designing reliable enterprise data systems. I bridge business operations and technical infrastructure through clean integrations and governed data pipelines.
about.bio2: This site is my public workspace: engineering notes, integration patterns, and tools I build along the way. It runs on <nextjs>Next.js</nextjs> + <payload>Payload CMS</payload> with a <supabase>Supabase</supabase> PostgreSQL backend.
about.bio3: Outside of work I'm probably debugging a flow, reading systems-design docs, or figuring out why the ETL ran 3 seconds slower than yesterday.
about.ctaText: Interested in working together or have an integration challenge?
about.ctaBlogNote: Or read what I've been <link>writing lately →</link>.
